name: Fortified gatehouse
description: Defensive gate structure: two square FillHollow towers at the ends of the AABB's long axis bracket a battlemented connecting wall. A 2-3 wide by 2 tall gate opening is punched through the middle of the wall and fitted with two @door blocks (one on each face). Battlements (alternating merlons + crenels) crown both towers and the wall; 4-6 @glass slits sit at mid-height in the tower walls; two minecraft:lantern blocks flank the gate at door-head height; a walkway of @floor blocks runs along the top of the connecting wall with @fence railings on both sides, joining the two towers end-to-end. Defensive on 5x6x3 to 14x10x5 AABBs (long x tall x short).

required_furniture.rule: Two FillHollow square towers at the two ends of the AABB's long axis; each is short_extent x height x short_extent, sharing the AABB y range.
FillHollow wall between the two towers, one tier shorter than the towers; same short-axis depth as a tower.
2-3 wide x 2 tall air opening at the bottom of the central wall, spanning the full wall thickness so it can be walked through.
Two @door blocks inside the opening, one on each outer face of the central wall.
Alternating @primary merlons and air crenels along one row above every tower top and along the wall top.
@glass blocks at mid-height in each tower's outer faces (end face + the two long-axis faces).
minecraft:lantern blocks on the outer face of the wall at door-head height, one on each side of the gate opening.
@floor strip running along the top of the connecting wall, with @fence railings every other column on both outer edges; connects the two towers.

placement_rules: Two towers flank a central gate
Towers occupy the two ends of the AABB's long axis; the wall fills the gap between them
Gate opening sits at the mid-length of the wall, 2-3 wide and 2 tall, punched all the way through the wall thickness
Doors are placed at y0 on each outer face of the wall, centred on the gate opening
Battlements crown both towers + wall in one continuous alternating merlon/crenel ring
Lanterns flank the gate on the outward-facing wall face at door-head height
Walkway connects towers at top — @floor planking with @fence railings on both long edges
Window slits sit at mid-height in the towers, never inside the gate face
lighting_ventilation: Two lanterns flanking the gate light the entry both day and night; tower window slits admit cross-light into the gatehouse interior. Battlements are open between merlons for archers; the gate opening itself is the main ventilation conduit.

medieval.notes: Classic stone-brick castle gatehouse with cobble footing, oak walkway and dark-oak doors. The canonical castle entry.

fantasy.notes: Dark blackstone keep gate with mossy footing and crimson-wood doors — wizard's stronghold flavour.

gothic.notes: Forbidding blackstone gate with nether-brick railings — fortress aesthetic, suited to a dark-fantasy castle.

viking.notes: Rough cobblestone gate with spruce walkway and doors — palisade-style holdfast.

modern.notes: Brutalist re-reading of a castle gate: smooth stone, iron bars and iron doors flanked by white-glass slits.

python_skill_signature: pipeline.skills.gatehouse.build(aabb: AABB, materials: Materials, style: str, **kwargs) -> list[Op]
style_affinity: Medieval / fortified-military motif. Coherent in medieval/castle/rustic/fantasy/gothic. Out of place in Modern, Minimalist, East-Asian, Classical/Renaissance buildings.
